space.description: The space of the view direction vector

viewdirection.description:
The current scene view direction, as defined by the shading environment.

The view direction is a normalized vector from the viewer position to the current shading position. In a PBR shading context, it represents the incident direction for primary camera rays, independent of any secondary or reflection rays.

faceforward.description: Makes the output always positive, facing towards the view direction

invert.description: Inverts the output values by multiplying them by -1

out.description: Output: the float representing the ratio between view direction and normal

facingratio.description:
The geometric facing ratio of the view direction and surface normal.

Facing ratio is computed as the dot product between the view direction and surface normal.

warm_color.description: The color facing toward the light

cool_color.description: The color facing away from the light

light_direction.description: The world-space direction of the light

out.description: Output: the Gooch lighting model result

gooch_shade.description:
Computes the color from single-pass shading portion of the Gooch[^Gooch1998] lighting model.

Gooch shade provides an illustrative shading effect by blending colors based on the angle between the surface normal and the light direction. It also provides a simple Phong specular highlight, on top of the warm and cool colors.

references.text: Gooch et al., A Non-Photorealistic Lighting Model For Automatic Technical Illustration, 1998.

energy_compensation.description: Enable energy compensation for the BSDF

oren_nayar_diffuse_bsdf.description:
Constructs a diffuse reflection BSDF based on the Oren-Nayar reflectance model.

A roughness of 0.0 gives Lambertian reflectance.

An energy_compensation boolean selects between the Qualitative Oren-Nayar[^Oren1994] and Energy-Preserving Oren-Nayar[^Portsmouth2025] models of diffuse reflectance.

references.text: Michael Oren, Shree K. Nayar, Generalization of Lambert’s Reflectance Model, 1994
Portsmouth et al., EON: A practical energy-preserving rough diffuse BRDF, 2025.

burley_diffuse_bsdf.description: Constructs a diffuse reflection BSDF based on the corresponding component of the Disney Principled model[^Burley2012].

references.text: Brent Burley, Physically-Based Shading at Disney, 2012

translucent_bsdf.description: Constructs a translucent (diffuse transmission) BSDF based on the Lambert reflectance model.

tint.description: Color weight to tint the reflected and transmitted light

ior.description: Index of refraction of the surface

roughness.description: Surface roughness along the tangent and bitangent

retroreflective.description: Enable retroreflection mode for the BSDF

thinfilm_thickness.description: Thickness of the iridescent thin-film layer in nanometers

thinfilm_ior.description: Index of refraction of the thin-film layer

scatter_mode.description: Surface Scatter mode, specifying reflection and/or transmission

dielectric_bsdf.description:
Constructs a reflection and/or transmission BSDF based on a microfacet reflectance model and a Fresnel curve for dielectrics[^Walter2007]. If reflection scattering is enabled the node may be layered vertically over a base BSDF for the surface beneath the dielectric layer. By chaining multiple <dielectric_bsdf> nodes you can describe a surface with multiple specular lobes. If transmission scattering is enabled the node may be layered over a VDF describing the surface interior to handle absorption and scattering inside the medium, useful for colored glass, turbid water, etc.

Implementations are expected to preserve energy as the roughness of the surface increases, with multiple scattering compensation[^Turquin2019] being a popular implementation strategy.

The tint input colors the reflected and transmitted light but should be left at white (1,1,1) for physically correct results. Setting the ior input to zero disables the Fresnel curve, allowing reflectivity to be controlled purely by weight and tint.

Setting retroreflective to true switches the BSDF to retroreflection mode, where light is reflected back toward the incoming direction rather than the mirror reflection direction[^Raab2025].

Thin-film iridescence effects[^Belcour2017] may be enabled by setting thinfilm_thickness to a non-zero value.

The scatter_mode controls whether the surface reflects light (R), transmits light (T), or both (RT). In RT mode, reflection and transmission occur both when entering and leaving a surface, with their respective intensities controlled by the Fresnel curve. Depending on the IOR and incident angle, total internal reflection may occur even when transmission modes are selected.

references.text: Bruce Walter et al., Microfacet Models for Refraction through Rough Surfaces, 2007
Emmanuel Turquin, Practical multiple scattering compensation for microfacet models, 2019.
Matthias Raab et al., The Minimal Retroreflective Microfacet Model, to appear, 2025
Laurent Belcour, Pascal Barla, A Practical Extension to Microfacet Theory for the Modeling of Varying Iridescence, 2017

retroreflective.description: Enable retroreflection mode for the BSDF

thinfilm_thickness.description: Thickness of the iridescent thin-film layer in nanometers

thinfilm_ior.description: Index of refraction of the thin-film layer

conductor_bsdf.description:
Constructs a reflection BSDF based on a microfacet reflectance model[^Burley2012]. Uses a Fresnel curve with complex refraction index for conductors/metals. If an artistic parametrization[^Gulbrandsen2014] is needed the [<artistic_ior>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-artistic-ior) utility node can be connected to handle this.

Implementations are expected to preserve energy as the roughness of the surface increases, with multiple scattering compensation[^Turquin2019] being a popular implementation strategy.

The default values for ior and extinction represent approximate values for gold.

Setting retroreflective to true switches the BSDF to retroreflection mode, where light is reflected back toward the incoming direction rather than the mirror reflection direction[^Raab2025].

Thin-film iridescence effects[^Belcour2017] may be enabled by setting thinfilm_thickness to a non-zero value.

references.text: Brent Burley, Physically-Based Shading at Disney, 2012
Ole Gulbrandsen, Artist Friendly Metallic Fresnel, 2014
Emmanuel Turquin, Practical multiple scattering compensation for microfacet models, 2019.
Matthias Raab et al., The Minimal Retroreflective Microfacet Model, to appear, 2025
Laurent Belcour, Pascal Barla, A Practical Extension to Microfacet Theory for the Modeling of Varying Iridescence, 2017

color0.description: Reflectivity per color component at facing angles

color90.description: Reflectivity per color component at grazing angles

exponent.description: Exponent for Schlick blending between color0 and color90

roughness.description: Surface roughness along the tangent and bitangent

retroreflective.description: Enable retroreflection mode for the BSDF

thinfilm_thickness.description: Thickness of the iridescent thin-film layer in nanometers

thinfilm_ior.description: Index of refraction of the thin-film layer

scatter_mode.description: Surface Scatter mode, specifying reflection and/or transmission

generalized_schlick_bsdf.description:
Constructs a reflection and/or transmission BSDF based on a microfacet model and a generalized Schlick Fresnel curve[^Hoffman2023]. If reflection scattering is enabled the node may be layered vertically over a base BSDF for the surface beneath the dielectric layer. By chaining multiple <generalized_schlick_bsdf> nodes you can describe a surface with multiple specular lobes. If transmission scattering is enabled the node may be layered over a VDF describing the surface interior to handle absorption and scattering inside the medium, useful for colored glass, turbid water, etc.

Implementations are expected to preserve energy as the roughness of the surface increases, with multiple scattering compensation[^Turquin2019] being a popular implementation strategy.

The color82 input provides a multiplier on reflectivity at 82 degrees, useful for capturing the characteristic "dip" in the reflectance curve of metallic surfaces. Setting it to (1,1,1) effectively disables this feature for backward compatibility.

Setting retroreflective to true switches the BSDF to retroreflection mode, where light is reflected back toward the incoming direction rather than the mirror reflection direction[^Raab2025].

Thin-film iridescence effects[^Belcour2017] may be enabled by setting thinfilm_thickness to a non-zero value.

The scatter_mode behavior matches that of dielectric_bsdf: in RT mode, reflection and transmission occur both when entering and leaving a surface, with intensities controlled by the Fresnel curve. Total internal reflection may occur depending on the incident angle.

references.text: Naty Hoffman, Generalization of Adobe's Fresnel Model,  2023
Emmanuel Turquin, Practical multiple scattering compensation for microfacet models, 2019.
Matthias Raab et al., The Minimal Retroreflective Microfacet Model, to appear, 2025
Laurent Belcour, Pascal Barla, A Practical Extension to Microfacet Theory for the Modeling of Varying Iridescence, 2017

radius.description: Mean free path per color channel

subsurface_bsdf.description:
Constructs a subsurface scattering BSDF for subsurface scattering within a homogeneous medium. The parameterization is chosen to match random walk Monte Carlo methods as well as approximate empirical methods[^Christensen2015]. Note that this category of subsurface scattering can be defined more rigorously as a BSDF vertically layered over an [<anisotropic_vdf>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-anisotropic-vdf), and we expect these two descriptions of the scattering-surface distribution function to be unified in future versions of MaterialX.

The radius input sets the average distance (mean free path) that light propagates below the surface before scattering back out, and can be set independently for each color channel.

The anisotropy input controls the scattering direction: negative values produce backwards scattering, positive values produce forward scattering, and zero produces uniform scattering.

references.text: Per H. Christensen, Brent Burley, Approximate Reflectance Profiles for Efficient Subsurface Scattering,  2015

mode.description: Selects between conty_kulla and zeltner sheen models

sheen_bsdf.description: Constructs a microfacet BSDF for the back-scattering properties of cloth-like materials. This node may be layered vertically over a base BSDF using a [<layer>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-layer) node. All energy that is not reflected will be transmitted to the base layer. A mode option selects between two available sheen models, Conty-Kulla[^Conty2017] and Zeltner[^Zeltner2022].

references.text: Alejandro Conty, Christopher Kulla, Production Friendly Microfacet Sheen BRDF, 2017
Tizian Zeltner et al., Practical Multiple-Scattering Sheen Using Linearly Transformed Cosines, 2022

tint_R.description: Color multiplier for the first R-lobe

tint_TT.description: Color multiplier for the first TT-lobe

tint_TRT.description: Color multiplier for the first TRT-lobe

roughness_R.description: Longitudinal and azimuthal roughness for R-lobe

roughness_TT.description: Longitudinal and azimuthal roughness for TT-lobe

roughness_TRT.description: Longitudinal and azimuthal roughness for TRT-lobe

absorption_coefficient.description: Absorption coefficient normalized to hair fiber diameter

chiang_hair_bsdf.description:
Constructs a hair BSDF based on the Chiang hair shading model[^Chiang2016]. This node does not support vertical layering.

The roughness inputs control longitudinal (ν) and azimuthal (s) roughness for each lobe, with (0,0) specifying pure specular scattering. The default ior of 1.55 represents the index of refraction for keratin. The cuticle_angle is in radians, with 0.5 representing no tilt, and values above 0.5 tilting the scales toward the root of the fiber.

references.text: Matt Jen-Yuan Chiang et al., A Practical and Controllable Hair and Fur Model for Production Path Tracing, 2016

color.description: Radiant emittance of light leaving the surface

uniform_edf.description: Constructs an EDF emitting light uniformly in all directions.

color.description: Radiant emittance of light leaving the surface

inner_angle.description: Angle of inner cone where intensity falloff starts

outer_angle.description: Angle of outer cone where intensity goes to zero

conical_edf.description:
Constructs an EDF emitting light inside a cone around the normal direction.

Light intensity begins to fall off at the inner_angle and reaches zero at the outer_angle (both specified in degrees). If the outer_angle is smaller than the inner_angle, no falloff occurs within the cone.

color.description: Radiant emittance of light leaving the surface

file.description: Path to a file containing IES light profile data

measured_edf.description: Constructs an EDF emitting light according to a measured IES light profile.

color0.description: Scale factor for emittance at facing angles

color90.description: Scale factor for emittance at grazing angles

exponent.description: Exponent for the Schlick blending between color0 and color90

base.description: The base EDF to be modified

generalized_schlick_edf.description: Adds a directionally varying factor to an EDF. Scales the emission distribution of the base EDF according to a generalized Schlick Fresnel curve.

absorption_vdf.description:
Constructs a VDF for pure light absorption.

The absorption input represents the absorption rate per distance traveled in the medium, stated in m<sup>−1</sup>, with independent control for each wavelength.

anisotropic_vdf.description:
Constructs a VDF scattering light for a participating medium, based on the Henyey-Greenstein phase function[^Pharr2023]. Forward, backward and uniform scattering is supported and controlled by the anisotropy input.

The absorption input represents the absorption rate per distance traveled in the medium, stated in m<sup>−1</sup>, with independent control for each wavelength.

The anisotropy input controls the scattering direction: negative values produce backwards scattering, positive values produce forward scattering, and 0.0 produces uniform scattering. Both absorption and scattering rates are specified per wavelength.

references.text: Matt Pharr et al., Physically Based Rendering: From Theory To Implementation, 2023

edf.description: Emission distribution function for the surface

thin_walled.description: Set to true to make the surface thin-walled

surface.description:
Constructs a surface shader describing light scattering and emission for surfaces. By default the node will construct a shader for a closed surface, representing an interface to a solid volume. In this mode refraction and scattering is enabled for any transmissive BSDFs connected to this surface. By setting thin_walled to "true" the node will instead construct a thin-walled surface, representing a surface with an infinitely thin volume. In thin-walled mode refraction and scattering will be disabled. Thin-walled mode must be enabled to construct a double-sided material with different surface shaders on the front and back side of geometry (using [<surfacematerial>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#node-surfacematerial) in the standard library).

If the edf input is left unconnected, no emission will occur from the surface.

vdf.description: Volume distribution function for the medium

edf.description: Emission distribution function for the medium

volume.description:
Constructs a volume shader describing a participating medium.

If the edf input is left unconnected, no emission will occur from the medium.

edf.description: Emission distribution function for the light source

intensity.description: Intensity multiplier for the EDF's emittance

exposure.description: Exposure control for the EDF's emittance

light.description: Constructs a light shader describing an explicit light source. The light shader will emit light according to the connected EDF. If the shader is attached to geometry both sides will be considered for light emission and the EDF controls if light is emitted from both sides or not.
light.notes: Note that the standard library includes definitions for [displacement](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#node-displacement) and [surface_unlit](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#node-surfaceunlit) shader nodes.

layer.description: Vertically layer a layerable BSDF such as [<dielectric_bsdf>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-dielectric-bsdf), [<generalized_schlick_bsdf>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-generalized-schlick-bsdf) or [<sheen_bsdf>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-sheen-bsdf) over a BSDF or VDF. The implementation is target specific, but a standard way of handling this is by albedo scaling, using the function "base*(1-reflectance(top)) + top", where the reflectance function calculates the directional albedo of a given BSDF.

mix.description: Mix two same-type distribution functions according to a weight. Performs horizontal layering by linear interpolation between the two inputs, using the function "bg∗(1−mix) + fg∗mix".

in2.description: The second distribution function

add.description: Additively blend two distribution functions of the same type.

in2.description: The scaling weight

multiply.description: Multiply the contribution of a distribution function by a scaling weight. The weight is either a float to attenuate the channels uniformly, or a color which can attenuate the channels separately. To be energy conserving the scaling weight should be no more than 1.0 in any channel.

roughness_anisotropy.description: Calculates anisotropic surface roughness from a scalar roughness and anisotropy parameterization. An anisotropy value above 0.0 stretches the roughness in the direction of the surface's "tangent" vector. An anisotropy value of 0.0 gives isotropic roughness. The roughness value is squared to achieve a more linear roughness look over the input range [0,1].

roughness.description: Roughness in x and y directions

roughness_dual.description: Calculates anisotropic surface roughness from a dual surface roughness parameterization. The roughness is squared to achieve a more linear roughness look over the input range [0,1].

glossiness_anisotropy.description: Calculates anisotropic surface roughness from a scalar glossiness and anisotropy parameterization. This node gives the same result as roughness anisotropy except that the glossiness value is an inverted roughness value. To be used as a convenience for shading models using the glossiness parameterization.

blackbody.description: Returns the radiant emittance of a blackbody radiator with the given temperature.

reflectivity.description: Reflectivity per color component at facing angles

edge_color.description: Reflectivity per color component at grazing angles

artistic_ior.description: Converts the artistic parameterization reflectivity and edge_color to complex IOR values. To be used with the [<conductor_bsdf>](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.PBRSpec.md#node-conductor-bsdf) node.

melanin_concentration.description: Amount of melanin affected to the output

melanin_redness.description: Amount of redness affected to the output

deon_hair_absorption_from_melanin.description: Converts the hair melanin parameterization to absorption coefficient based on pigments eumelanin and pheomelanin using the mapping method described in [^d'Eon2011]. The default of eumelanin_color and pheomelanin_color are lin_rec709 color converted from the constants[^d'Eon2011] via exp(-c). They may be transformed to scene-linear rendering color space.

references.text: Eugene d'Eon et al., An Energy-Conserving Hair Reflectance Model, 2011

chiang_hair_absorption_from_color.description: Converts the hair scattering color to absorption coefficient using the mapping method described in [^Chiang2016].

references.text: Matt Jen-Yuan Chiang et al., A Practical and Controllable Hair and Fur Model for Production Path Tracing, 2016

chiang_hair_roughness.description: Converts the artistic parameterization hair roughness to roughness for R, TT and TRT lobes, as described in [^Chiang2016].

references.text: Matt Jen-Yuan Chiang et al., A Practical and Controllable Hair and Fur Model for Production Path Tracing, 2016
Stephen R. Marschner et al., Light Scattering from Human Hair Fibers, 2003

surface_unlit.description: An unlit surface shader node, representing a surface that can emit and transmit light, but does not receive illumination from light sources or other surfaces.

out.description: Output: true for any non-zero input value
Output: copy input value to all channels
Output: an unlit surface shader emitting the input value as color

convert.description: Convert a stream from one data type to another.
convert.notes:
For boolean input values, all numeric output values will be either zero or one.

For colorN/vectorN to colorM/vectorM:

* if N is the same as M, then channels are directly copied. * if N is larger than M, then the first M channels are used and the excess channels ignored. * if N is smaller than M, then the N channels are directly copied and additional channels are populated with 0, aside from the fourth channel which is populated with 1.

file.description: The URI of the image file

layer.description: The name of the layer to extract from a multi-layer input file

default.description: A default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the image data is read

uaddressmode.description: Determines how U coordinates outside the 0-1 range are processed before sampling the image

vaddressmode.description: Determines how V coordinates outside the 0-1 range are processed before sampling the image

filtertype.description: The type of texture filtering to use

framerange.description: A string "minframe-maxframe", e.g. "10-99", to specify the range of frames that the image file is allowed to have

frameoffset.description: A number that is added to the current frame number to get the image file frame number

frameendaction.description: What to do when the resolved image frame number is outside the framerange range

image.description:
Samples data from a single image, or from a layer within a multi-layer image.  When used in the context of rendering a geometry, the image is mapped onto the geometry based on geometry UV coordinates, with the lower-left corner of an image mapping to the (0,0) UV coordinate (or to the fractional (0,0) UV coordinate for tiled images).

The type of the <image> node determines the number of channels output, which may be less than the number of channels in the image file, outputting the first N channels from the image file.  So a float <image> would return the Red channel of an RGB image, and a color3 <image> would return the RGB channels of an RGBA image.  If the type of the <image> node has more channels than the referenced image file, then the output will contain zero values in all channels beyond the N channels of the image file.

The file input value can include one or more substitutions to change the file name that is accessed, as described in the [Filename Substitutions](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#filename-substitutions) section in the main Specification document.

If no value for layer is provided and the input file has multiple layers, then the "default" layer will be used, or "rgba" if there is no "default" layer. Note: the number of channels defined by the type of the image must match the number of channels in the named layer.

The default input is the default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved (e.g. if a geometry token, interface token, or hostattr is included in the filename but no substitution value or default is defined, or if the resolved file URI cannot be read), or if the specified layer does not exist in the file. The default value must be the same type as the <image> element itself. If default is not defined, the default color value will be 0.0 in all channels.

file.description: The URI of the image file

default.description: A default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the image data is read

uvtiling.description: The tiling rate for the given image along the U and V axes

uvoffset.description: The offset for the given image along the U and V axes

realworldimagesize.description: The real-world size represented by the file image

realworldtilesize.description: The real-world size of a single square 0-1 UV tile

filtertype.description: The type of texture filtering to use

framerange.description: A string "minframe-maxframe", e.g. "10-99", to specify the range of frames that the image file is allowed to have

frameoffset.description: A number that is added to the current frame number to get the image file frame number

frameendaction.description: What to do when the resolved image frame number is outside the framerange range

tiledimage.description:
Samples data from a single image, with provisions for tiling and offsetting the image across UV space.

The file input can include one or more substitutions to change the file name that is accessed, as described in the [Filename Substitutions](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#filename-substitutions) section in the main Specification document.

file.description: The URI of the image file

default.description: A default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved

viewdir.description: The view direction determining the value sampled from the projected equiangular map

rotation.description: The longitudinal sampling offset, in degrees

latlongimage.description:
Samples an equiangular map along a view direction with adjustable latitudinal offset.

The file input can include one or more substitutions to change the file name that is accessed, as described in the [Filename Substitutions](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#filename-substitutions) section in the main Specification document.

file.description: The URI of the image file

default.description: A default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the image data is read

tiling.description: The tiling rate for the given image along the U and V axes

rotationrange.description: Range in degrees used to randomize rotation for each tile

scale.description: Per-tile scale randomness multiplier applied to tile size

scalerange.description: Range of scale multipliers used to randomize tile scale

offset.description: Per-tile translation randomness in UV units

offsetrange.description: Range of offset values in UV units used to randomize tile positions

falloff.description: Falloff width used to blend neighboring tiles at their edges; larger values produce smoother blends

falloffcontrast.description: Contrast applied to the falloff blending to sharpen (values >1) or soften (values <1) transitions

lumacoeffs.description: The luma coefficients of the current working color space

hextiledimage.description:
Samples data from a single image, with provisions for hex-tiling and randomizing the image across UV space.

The file input can include one or more substitutions to change the file name that is accessed, as described in the [Filename Substitutions](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#filename-substitutions) section in the main Specification document.

The lumacoeffs input represents the luma coefficients of the current working color space. If no specific color space can be determined, the ACEScg (ap1) luma coefficients [0.2722287, 0.6740818, 0.0536895] will be used. Applications which support color management systems may choose to retrieve the luma coefficients of the working colorspace from the CMS to pass to the node's implementation directly, rather than exposing it to the user.

filex.description: The URI of the image file to be projected in the direction from the +X axis back toward the origin

filey.description: The URI of the image file to be projected in the direction from the +Y axis back toward the origin

filez.description: The URI of the image file to be projected in the direction from the +Z axis back toward the origin

layerx.description: The name of the layer to extract from a multi-layer input file for the X-axis projection

layery.description: The name of the layer to extract from a multi-layer input file for the Y-axis projection

layerz.description: The name of the layer to extract from a multi-layer input file for the Z-axis projection

default.description: A default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved

position.description: A spatially-varying input specifying the 3D position at which the projection is evaluated

normal.description: A spatially-varying input specifying the 3D normal vector used for blending

upaxis.description: Which axis is considered to be 'up', either 0 for X, 1 for Y, or 2 for Z

blend.description: Weighting factor for blending samples using the geometric normal, with higher values giving softer blending

filtertype.description: The type of texture filtering to use

framerange.description: A string "minframe-maxframe", e.g. "10-99", to specify the range of frames that the image file is allowed to have

frameoffset.description: A number that is added to the current frame number to get the image file frame number

frameendaction.description: What to do when the resolved image frame number is outside the framerange range

triplanarprojection.description: Samples data from three images (or layers within multi-layer images), and projects a tiled representation of the images along each of the three respective coordinate axes, computing a weighted blend of the three samples using the geometric normal.

value.description: The value that will be sent to out
The value that will be sent to out
The value that will be sent to out

randomfloat.description: Produces a stable randomized float value between min and max, based on an input signal and optional seed value.  Uses a 2d cellnoise function to produce the output.

texcoord.description: 2D texture coordinate at which the ramp interpolation is evaluated
2D texture coordinate at which the ramp interpolation is evaluated

valuetl.description: Value at the top-left (U=0, V=1) corner

valuetr.description: Value at the top-right (U=1, V=1) corner

valuebl.description: Value at the bottom-left (U=0, V=0) corner

valuebr.description: Value at the bottom-right (U=1, V=0) corner

texcoord.description: 2D texture coordinate at which the ramp interpolation is evaluated

center.description: U-coordinate of the split; left of it is valuel, right is valuer

texcoord.description: 2D texture coordinate at which the ramp interpolation is evaluated

splitlr.description: A left-right split matte, split at a specified U value.

center.description: V-coordinate of the split; below it is valueb, above is valuet

texcoord.description: 2D texture coordinate at which the ramp interpolation is evaluated

splittb.description: A top-bottom split matte, split at a specified V value.

amplitude.description: The center-to-peak amplitude of the noise

pivot.description: The center value of the output noise

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the noise is evaluated

amplitude.description: The center-to-peak amplitude of the noise

octaves.description: The number of octaves of noise to be summed

lacunarity.description: The exponential scale between successive octaves of noise

diminish.description: The rate at which noise amplitude is diminished for each octave

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the noise is evaluated

fractal2d.description: Zero-centered 2D Fractal noise in 1, 2, 3 or 4 channels, created by summing several octaves of 2D Perlin noise, increasing the frequency and decreasing the amplitude at each octave.

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the noise is evaluated

cellnoise2d.description: 2D cellular noise, 1 or 3 channels (type float or vector3).

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the noise is evaluated

jitter.description: The amount to jitter the cell center position

worleynoise2d.description: 2D Worley noise using centered jitter, outputting float (distance metric to closest feature), vector2 (distance metrics to closest 2 features) or vector3 (distance metrics to closest 3 features) values.

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the noise is evaluated

freq.description: The noise frequency, with higher values producing smaller noise shapes.

jitter.description: The amount to jitter the cell center position

clampoutput.description: If enabled the output is clamped between the min and max output values

octaves.description: The number of octaves of noise to be summed

lacunarity.description: The exponential scale between successive octaves of noise

diminish.description: The rate at which noise amplitude is diminished for each octave

type.description: The type of noise function to use.  One of 0 (Perlin), 1 (Cell), 2 (Worley), or 3 (Fractal)

unifiednoise2d.description: A single node supporting 2D Perlin, Cell, Worley or Fractal noise in a unified interface.

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the flake pattern is evaluated

size.description: The size of individual flakes, with smaller values producing larger flakes

roughness.description: The surface roughness of individual flakes, controlling the variation in normal

coverage.description: The density of flakes in the pattern, ranging from 0.0 (no flakes) to 1.0 (maximum)

normal.description: The surface normal vector used as the base for flake normal perturbations

tangent.description: The surface tangent vector, used to construct the tangent space

bitangent.description: The surface bitangent vector, used to construct the tangent space

id.description: Output: unique identifier for each flake. 0 for no flake

rand.description: Output: random value per flake for additional variation. 0.0 for no flake

presence.description: Output: presence per flake; a depth-like value (higher is closer to the surface). 0.0 for no flake.

flakenormal.description: Output: the computed flake normal. Base normal if no flake present

flake2d.description: Generates a procedural flake pattern in 2D space, suitable for simulating metallic flakes in materials such as car paint.

color1.description: The first color used in the checkerboard pattern.

color2.description: The second color used in the checkerboard pattern.

uvtiling.description: The tiling of the checkerboard pattern along each axis, with higher values producing smaller squares.

uvoffset.description: The offset of the checkerboard pattern along each axis

texcoord.description: The input 2d space.
The input 2d space

center.description: An offset value added to both the point1 and point2 coordinates

radius.description: The radius or 'half thickness' of the line

point1.description: The UV coordinate of the first endpoint

point2.description: The UV coordinate of the second endpoint

line.description: 2D line pattern: outputs 1 if texcoord is at less than radius distance from a line segment defined by point1 and point2, otherwise outputs 0.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

center.description: The center coordinate of the circle

circle.description: 2D circle (disk) pattern: outputs 1 if texcoord is inside a circle defined by center and radius, otherwise outputs 0.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

center.description: The center coordinate of the cloverleaf

cloverleaf.description: 2D cloverleaf pattern: four semicircles on the edges of a square defined by center and radius.  Outputs 1 if texcoord is within the pattern, otherwise outputs 0.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

center.description: The center coordinate of the hexagon

hexagon.description: 2D hexagon pattern: outputs 1 if texcoord is inside a hexagon shape inscribed by a circle defined by center and radius; otherwise outputs 0.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

uvtiling.description: Tiling factor, with higher values producing a denser grid.

thickness.description: The thickness of the grid lines

staggered.description: If true, every other row will be offset 50% to produce a 'brick wall' pattern

grid.description: Creates a grid pattern of (1, 1, 1) white lines on a (0, 0, 0) black background with the given tiling, offset, and line thickness. Pattern can be regular or staggered.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

uvtiling.description: Tiling factor, with higher values producing a denser grid.

thickness.description: The thickness of the grid lines

staggered.description: If true, every other row will be offset 50% to produce an 'alternating diamond' pattern

crosshatch.description: Creates a crosshatch pattern with the given tiling, offset, and line thickness. Pattern can be regular or staggered.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

uvtiling.description: Tiling factor, with higher values producing a denser grid.

size.description: The diameter of the circles in the tiled pattern. If size is 1.0, the edges of adjacent circles in the tiling will exactly touch.

staggered.description: If true, every other row will be offset 50%, and the spacing of the tiling will be adjusted in the V direction to center the circles on the vertices of an equilateral triangle grid

tiledcircles.description: Creates a black and white pattern of circles with a defined tiling and size (diameter). Pattern can be regular or staggered.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

uvtiling.description: Tiling factor, with higher values producing a denser grid.

size.description: The outer diameter of the cloverleafs in the tiled pattern. If size is 1.0, the edges of adjacent cloverleafs in the tiling will exactly touch.

staggered.description: If true, an additional pattern of cloverleafs will be generated in between the originals offset by 50% in both U and V

tiledcloverleafs.description: Creates a black and white pattern of cloverleafs with a defined tiling and size (diameter of the circles circumscribing the shape). Pattern can be regular or staggered.

texcoord.description: The input 2d space

uvtiling.description: Tiling factor, with higher values producing a denser grid.

size.description: The inner diameter of the hexagons in the tiled pattern. if size is 1.0, the edges of adjacent hexagons in the U-direcction tiling will exactly touch

staggered.description: If true, every other row will be offset 50%, and the spacing of the tiling will be adjusted in the V direction to center the hexagons on the vertices of an equilateral triangle grid.

tiledhexagons.description: Creates a black and white pattern of hexagons with a defined tiling and size (diameter of the circles circumscribing the shape). Pattern can be regular or staggered.

amplitude.description: The center-to-peak amplitude of the noise

pivot.description: The center value of the output noise

position.description: The 3D position at which the noise is evaluated

amplitude.description: The center-to-peak amplitude of the noise

octaves.description: The number of octaves of noise to be summed

lacunarity.description: The exponential scale between successive octaves of noise

diminish.description: The rate at which noise amplitude is diminished for each octave

position.description: The 3D position at which the noise is evaluated

fractal3d.description: Zero-centered 3D Fractal noise in 1, 2, 3 or 4 channels, created by summing several octaves of 3D Perlin noise, increasing the frequency and decreasing the amplitude at each octave.

position.description: The 3D position at which the noise is evaluated

cellnoise3d.description: 3D cellular noise, 1 or 3 channels (type float or vector3).

position.description: The 3D position at which the noise is evaluated

jitter.description: The amount to jitter the cell center position

worleynoise3d.description: 3D Worley noise using centered jitter, outputting float (distance metric to closest feature), vector2 (distance metrics to closest 2 features) or vector3 (distance metrics to closest 3 features) values.

position.description: The 3D position at which the noise is evaluated

freq.description: The noise frequency, with higher values producing smaller noise shapes.

jitter.description: The amount to jitter the cell center position

clampoutput.description: If enabled the output is clamped between the min and max output values

octaves.description: The number of octaves of noise to be summed

lacunarity.description: The exponential scale between successive octaves of noise

diminish.description: The rate at which noise amplitude is diminished for each octave

type.description: The type of noise function to use.  One of 0 (Perlin), 1 (Cell), 2 (Worley), or 3 (Fractal)

unifiednoise3d.description: A single node supporting 3D Perlin, Cell, Worley or Fractal noise in a unified interface.

position.description: The 3D position at which the flake pattern is evaluated

size.description: The size of individual flakes, with smaller values producing larger flakes

roughness.description: The surface roughness of individual flakes, controlling the variation in normal

coverage.description: The density of flakes in the pattern, ranging from 0.0 (no flakes) to 1.0 (maximum)

normal.description: The surface normal vector used as the base for flake normal perturbations

tangent.description: The surface tangent vector, used to construct the tangent space

bitangent.description: The surface bitangent vector, used to construct the tangent space

id.description: Output: unique identifier for each flake. 0 for no flake

rand.description: Output: random value per flake for additional variation. 0.0 for no flake

presence.description: Output: presence per flake; a depth-like value (higher is closer to the surface). 0.0 for no flake.

flakenormal.description: Output: the computed flake normal. Base normal if no flake present

flake3d.description: Generates a procedural flake pattern in 3D space, suitable for simulating metallic flakes in materials such as car paint.

out.description: Output: the randomized RGB color value

randomcolor.description: Produces a randomized RGB color within a randomized hue, saturation and brightness range, based on an input signal and optional seed value.  Output type color3.

space.description: The coordinate space of the output position

position.description: The coordinates associated with the currently-processed data, as defined in a specific coordinate space.

space.description: The coordinate space of the output position

normal.description: The normalized geometric normal associated with the currently-processed data, as defined in a specific coordinate space.

space.description: The coordinate space of the output position

index.description: The index of the texcoord space to use to compute the tangent vector

out.description: Output: the tangent vector associated with the indexth coordinate space, in space

tangent.description: The geometric tangent vector associated with the currently-processed data, as defined in a specific coordinate space.

space.description: The coordinate space of the output position

index.description: The index of the texcoord space to use to compute the bitangent vector

out.description: Output: the bitangent vector associated with the indexth coordinate space, in space

bitangent.description: The geometric bi-tangent vector associated with the currently-processed data, as defined in a specific coordinate space.

texcoord.description: The 2D or 3D texture coordinates associated with the currently-processed data

geomcolor.description: The color associated with the current geometry at the current position, generally bound via per-vertex color values. The type must match the type of the "color" bound to the geometry.

geomprop.description: The geometric property to be referenced

default.description: A value to return if the specified geomprop is not defined on the current geometry

geompropvalue.description: The value of the specified varying geometric property (defined by a <geompropdef>) of the currently-bound geometry. This node's type must match that of the referenced geomprop.

geomprop.description: The geometric property to be referenced

default.description: A value to return if the specified geomprop is not defined on the current geometry

out.description: Output: A value to return if the specified geomprop is not defined on the current geometry

geompropvalueuniform.description: The value of the specified uniform geometric property (defined by a <geompropdef>) of the currently-bound geometry. This node's type must match that of the referenced geomprop.

height.description: Amount to offset the surface normal.

scale.description: Scalar to adjust the height amount.

bump.description: The normalized normal computed by offsetting the surface world space position along its world space normal.

out.description: Output: frame number as defined by the host environment

frame.description: The current frame number as defined by the host environment.

out.description: Output: current time in seconds as defined by the host environment

time.description: The current time in seconds, as defined by the host environment.

in2.description: The stream to add to in1

out.description: Output: sum of in1 and in2

in2.description: The stream to add to in1

out.description: Output: sum of in1 and in2

in2.description: The stream to add to in1

out.description: Output: sum of in1 and in2

add.description: Add a value to the incoming float/color/vector/matrix

in2.description: The stream to subtract from in1
The stream to subtract from in1
The stream to subtract from in1

subtract.description: Subtract a value from the incoming float/color/vector/matrix

in2.description: The stream to multiply in1 by

out.description: Output: product of in1 and in2

in2.description: The stream to multiply in1 by

out.description: Output: product of in1 and in2

multiply.description: Multiply two values together. Scalar and vector types multiply component-wise, while matrices multiply using a standard matrix product.

in2.description: The stream to divide in1 by
The stream to divide in1 by

divide.description: Divide one value by another. Scalar and vector types divide component-wise, while for matrices in1 is multiplied by the inverse of in2.

in2.description: The stream to modulo in1 by

modulo.description: The remaining fraction after dividing an incoming float/color/vector by a value and subtracting the integer portion. Modulo always returns a non-negative result.

out.description: Output: The fractional part of in

fract.description: Returns the fractional part of the floating-point input.

amount.description: The value to subtract in from

invert.description: Subtract the incoming float, color, or vector from amount in all channels, outputting: amount - in.

absval.description: The per-channel absolute value of the incoming float/color/vector.

out.description: Output: nearest integer less than or equal to in
Output: nearest integer less than or equal to in

floor.description: The per-channel nearest integer value less than or equal to the incoming float/color/vector. The output remains in floating point per-channel, i.e. the same type as the input, except that <floor> of a float input also has a variant outputting an integer type.

out.description: Output: nearest integer greater than or equal to in
Output: nearest integer greater than or equal to in

ceil.description: The per-channel nearest integer value greater than or equal to the incoming float/color/vector. The output remains in floating point per-channel, i.e. the same type as the input, except that <ceil> of a float input also has a variant outputting an integer type.

out.description: Output: in rounded to the nearest integer
Output: in rounded to the nearest integer

round.description: Round each channel of the incoming float/color/vector values to the nearest integer value.

in2.description: The exponent to raise in1 to

power.description: Raise incoming float/color values to the specified exponent, commonly used for "gamma" adjustment.

in2.description: The exponent to raise in1 to

safepower.description: Raise incoming float/color values to the specified exponent. Negative "in1" values will result in negative output values.

sin.description: The sine of the incoming value, which is expected to be expressed in radians.

cos.description: The cosine of the incoming value, which is expected to be expressed in radians.

tan.description: The tangent of the incoming value, which is expected to be expressed in radians.

asin.description: The arcsine of the incoming value. The output will be expressed in radians.

acos.description: The arccosine of the incoming value. The output will be expressed in radians.

iny.description: Vertical component of the point to which the the angle is to be found

inx.description: Horizontal component of the point to which the angle is to be found

out.description: Output: angle relative to the X-axis of the line joining the origin and the point (inx, iny)

atan2.description: The arctangent of the expression (iny/inx). The output will be expressed in radians.

sqrt.description: The square root of the incoming value.

ln.description: The natural logarithm of the incoming value.

exp.description: $e$ to the power of the incoming value.

sign.description: The per-channel sign of the incoming float/color/vector value: -1 for negative, +1 for positive, or 0 for zero.

in.description: The input stream to be clamped

low.description: Any value of in lower than this value will be set to this value

high.description: Any value of in higher than this value will be set to this value

clamp.description: Clamp incoming values per-channel to a specified range of float/color/vector values.

in2.description: The second input stream

out.description: Output: minimum of in1 and in2

min.description: Select the minimum of the two incoming values.

in2.description: The second input stream

out.description: Output: maximum of in1 and in2

max.description: Select the maximum of the two incoming values.

normalize.description: Output the incoming vectorN stream normalized.

magnitude.description: Output the float magnitude (vector length) of the incoming vectorN stream; cannot be used on float or colorN streams. Note: the fourth channel in vector4 streams is not treated any differently, e.g. not as a homogeneous "w" value.

in2.description: Point to calculate distance to

out.description: Output: distance between in1 and in2

distance.description: Measures the distance between two points in 2D, 3D, or 4D.

in2.description: The second input vector stream

out.description: Output: dot product of in1 and in2

dotproduct.description: Output the (float) dot product of two incoming vectorN streams; cannot be used on float or colorN streams.

in2.description: The second input vector stream

out.description: Output: cross product of in1 and in2

crossproduct.description: Output the (vector3) cross product of two incoming vector3 streams; cannot be used on any other stream type. A disabled crossproduct node passes through the value of in1 unchanged.

fromspace.description: The name of a vector space understood by the rendering target to transform the in point from; may be empty to specify the renderer's working space.

tospace.description: The name of a vector space understood by the rendering target for the space to transform the in point to.

out.description: Output: point transformed from fromspace to tospace

transformpoint.description: Transform the incoming vector3 coordinate from one specified space to another; cannot be used on any other stream type.

fromspace.description: The name of a vector space understood by the rendering target to transform the in vector from; may be empty to specify the renderer's working space.

tospace.description: The name of a vector space understood by the rendering target for the space to transform the in vector to.

out.description: Output: point transformed from fromspace to tospace

transformvector.description: Transform the incoming vector3 coordinate from one specified space to another; cannot be used on any other stream type.

fromspace.description: The name of a vector space understood by the rendering target to transform the in normal from; may be empty to specify the renderer's working space.

tospace.description: The name of a vector space understood by the rendering target for the space to transform the in normal to.

out.description: Output: point transformed from fromspace to tospace

transformnormal.description: Transform the incoming vector3 normal from one specified space to another; cannot be used on any other stream type.

transformmatrix.description: Transform the incoming vectorN by the specified matrix.

in.description: Input normal in space of frame defined by normal, tangent, bitangent ports

scale.description: Scaling factor to apply to the normal

normal.description: Normal of the local frame from which to transform in

tangent.description: Tangent of the local frame from which to transform in

bitangent.description: Bitangent of the local frame from which to transform in

out.description: Output: Ouptut normal in world space

normalmap.description: Transform a normal vector from the encoded tangent space to world space. The input normal vector is assumed to be encoded with all channels in the [0-1] range, as would commonly be output from a normal map.

file.description: The URI of the image file

default.description: A default value to use if the file reference can not be resolved

texcoord.description: The 2D texture coordinate at which the image data is read

tiling.description: The tiling rate for the given image along the U and V axes

rotationrange.description: Range in degrees used to randomize rotation for each tile

scale.description: Per-tile scale randomness multiplier applied to tile size

scalerange.description: Range of scale multipliers used to randomize tile scale

offset.description: Per-tile translation randomness in UV units

offsetrange.description: Range of offset values in UV units used to randomize tile positions

falloff.description: Falloff width used to blend neighboring tiles at their edges; larger values produce smoother blends

strength.description: Controls how strongly the sampled normal map affects the final normal.

flip_g.description: If true, negate the green channel of the sampled normal map to accommodate tangent-space conventions

out.description: Output: the sampled normal map value

hextilednormalmap.description:
Samples data from a single normalmap, with provisions for hex-tiling and randomizing the normalmap across UV space.

The file input can include one or more substitutions to change the file name that is accessed, as described in the [Filename Substitutions](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.Specification.md#filename-substitutions) section in the main Specification document.

The strength input controls how strongly the sampled normal map affects the final normal. A value of 0.0 leaves the surface normal unchanged, 1.0 applies the sampled normal at full strength, and values >1.0 amplify the normal perturbation.

determinant.description: Output the determinant of the incoming matrix.

amount.description: The angle to rotate, specified in degrees. Positive values rotate counter-clockwise

rotate2d.description: Rotate the incoming 2D vector about the origin.

amount.description: The angle to rotate, specified in degrees. Positive values rotate counter-clockwise

axis.description: The unit axis vector to rotate in around

rotate3d.description: Rotate the incoming 3D vector about the specified unit axis vector.

texcoord.description: Input texture coordinates to transform

pivot.description: Pivot point around which to rotate and scale texcoord

scale.description: Scaling factor to apply to in

rotate.description: Amount to rotate in, in degrees

operationorder.description: The order in which transform operations are performed

place2d.description:
Transform incoming 2D texture coordinates from one frame of reference to another.

The operationorder input controls the order in which transform operations are performed. The SRT option performs -pivot, scale, rotate, translate, +pivot. The TRS option performs -pivot, translate, rotate, scale, +pivot.

trianglewave.description: Generate a triangle wave from the given scalar input. The generated wave ranges from zero to one and repeats on integer boundaries.

normal.description: Vector normal to the surface about which to reflect in

out.description: Output: reflection of in about normal

reflect.description: Reflect the incoming 3D vector about a surface normal vector.

normal.description: Vector normal to the surface through which to refract in

ior.description: The relative index of refraction of the interior of the surface to the exterior

out.description: Output: refraction of in through normal

refract.description: Refract the incoming 3D vector through a surface with the given surface normal and relative index of refraction.

in2.description: The second row of out

in1.description: The first row of out, appended with 0

in2.description: The second row of out, appended with 0

in3.description: The third row of out, appended with 0

in4.description: The fourth row of out, appended with 1

in2.description: The second row of out

creatematrix.description: Build a 3x3 or 4x4 matrix from three vector3 or four vector3 or vector4 inputs. A matrix44 may also be created from vector3 input values, in which case the fourth value will be set to 0.0 for in1-in3, and to 1.0 for in4 when creating the matrix44.

in.description: The input stream to be adjusted

inlow.description: Low value for the input range

inhigh.description: High value for the input range

outlow.description: Low value for the output range

outhigh.description: High value for the output range

remap.description: Linearly remap incoming values from one range of values [inlow, inhigh] to another [outlow, outhigh].

in.description: The input value to the smoothstep function

low.description: Low value for the input range

high.description: Low value for the output range

smoothstep.description: Output a smooth, hermite-interpolated remapping of input values from [low, high] to [0,1].

in.description: The input color stream to be converted

lumacoeffs.description: The luma coefficients of the current working color space

luminance.description:
Output a grayscale value containing the luminance of the incoming RGB color in all color channels.

The lumacoeffs input represents the luma coefficients of the current working color space. If no specific color space can be determined, the ACEScg (ap1) luma coefficients [0.2722287, 0.6740818, 0.0536895] will be used. Applications which support color management systems may choose to retrieve the luma coefficients of the working colorspace from the CMS to pass to the <luminance> node's implementation directly, rather than exposing it to the user.

in.description: The input color stream to be converted

out.description: Output: in converted from RGB to HSV

rgbtohsv.description: Convert an incoming color from RGB to HSV space (with H and S ranging from 0 to 1); the alpha channel is left unchanged if present. This conversion is not affected by the current color space.

in.description: The input color stream to be converted

out.description: Output: in converted from HSV to RGB

hsvtorgb.description: Convert an incoming color from HSV to RGB space; the alpha channel is left unchanged if present. This conversion is not affected by the current color space.

in.description: The input color stream to be adjusted

amount.description: Slope multiplier for contrast adjustment. Values greater than 1.0 increase contrast, values between 0.0 and 1.0 reduce contrast.

pivot.description: Center pivot value of contrast adjustment; this is the value that will not change as contrast is adjusted.

contrast.description: Increase or decrease the contrast of the incoming in values using amount as a linear slope multiplier.

in.description: The input stream to be adjusted

inlow.description: Low value for the input range

inhigh.description: High value for the input range

gamma.description: Reciprocal of the exponent applied to the remapped input

outlow.description: Low value for the output range

outhigh.description: High value for the output range

doclamp.description: If true, the output is clamped to [outlow, outhigh]

range.description: Remap incoming values from one range of values to another, optionally applying a gamma correction "in the middle".

in.description: The input color stream to be adjusted

amount.description: Hue offset, saturation scale, and luminance scale in (x, y, z), respectively

hsvadjust.description:
Adjust the hue, saturation and value of an RGB color by converting the input color to HSV, adding amount.x to the hue, multiplying the saturation by amount.y, multiplying the value by amount.z, then converting back to RGB.

Positive amount.x values rotate hue in the "red to green to blue" direction, with amount.x of 1.0 being the equivalent to a 360 degree (e.g. no-op) rotation.  Negative or greater-than-1.0 hue adjustment values are allowed, wrapping at the 0-1 boundaries.  The internal conversions between RGB and HSV spaces are not affected by the current color space, and for color4 inputs, the alpha value is left unchanged.

in.description: The input color stream to be adjusted

lumacoeffs.description: The luma coefficients to use to calculate the desaturated value

saturate.description:
Adjust the saturation of a color, the alpha channel will be unchanged if present.

The lumacoeffs input represents the luma coefficients of the current working color space. If no specific color space can be determined, the ACEScg (ap1) luma coefficients [0.2722287, 0.6740818, 0.0536895] will be used. Applications which support color management systems may choose to retrieve the luma coefficients of the working colorspace from the CMS to pass to the node's implementation directly, rather than exposing it to the user.

saturation.description: Multiplies the input color saturation level

gamma.description: Applies a gamma correction to the color

lift.description: Raises the dark color values, leaving the white values unchanged

gain.description: Multiplier increases lighter color values, leaving black values unchanged

contrast.description: Linearly increase or decrease the color contrast

contrastpivot.description: Pivot value around which contrast applies

colorcorrect.description: Combines various adjustment nodes into one artist-friendly color correction node. For color4 inputs, the alpha value is unchanged.

in.description: The input stream to be premultiplied

premult.description: Multiply the RGB channels of the input by the Alpha channel of the input.

in.description: The input stream to be unpremultiplied

unpremult.description: Divide the RGB channels of the input by the Alpha channel of the input. If the Alpha value is zero, the in value is passed through unchanged.

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'plus' operation (mix=1)

plus.description: Add two 1-4 channel inputs, with optional mixing between the bg input and the result.

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'minus' operation (mix=1)

minus.description: Subtract two 1-4 channel inputs, with optional mixing between the bg input and the result.

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'difference' operation (mix=1)

difference.description: Absolute-value difference of two 1-4 channel inputs, with optional mixing between the bg input and the result.

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'burn' operation (mix=1)

burn.description:
Take two 1-4 channel inputs and apply the same operator to all channels:

1-(1-B)/F

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'dodge' operation (mix=1)

dodge.description:
Take two 1-4 channel inputs and apply the same operator to all channels:

B/(1-F)

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'screen' operation (mix=1)

screen.description:
Take two 1-4 channel inputs and apply the same operator to all channels:

1-(1-F)*(1-B)

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'overlay' operation (mix=1)

overlay.description:
Take two 1-4 channel inputs and apply the same operator to all channels:

2FB if B<0.5; 1-2(1-F)(1-B) if B>=0.5

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'disjointover' operation (mix=1)

disjointover.description:
Take two color4 inputs and use the built-in alpha channel(s) to control the compositing of the fg and bg inputs:

F+B         if f+b<=1 F+B(1-f)/b  if f+b>1 alpha: min(f+b,1)

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'in' operation (mix=1)

in.description:
Take two color4 inputs and use the built-in alpha channel(s) to control the compositing of the fg and bg inputs:

RGB = Fb Alpha = fb

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'mask' operation (mix=1)

mask.description:
Take two color4 inputs and use the built-in alpha channel(s) to control the compositing of the fg and bg inputs:

RGB = Bf Alpha = bf

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'matte' operation (mix=1)

matte.description:
Take two color4 inputs and use the built-in alpha channel(s) to control the compositing of the fg and bg inputs:

RGB = Ff+B(1-f) Alpha = f+b(1-f)

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'out' operation (mix=1)

out.description:
Take two color4 inputs and use the built-in alpha channel(s) to control the compositing of the fg and bg inputs:

RGB = F(1-b) Alpha = f(1-b)

mix.description: A mixing value between bg (mix=0) and the result of the 'over' operation (mix=1)

over.description:
Take two color4 inputs and use the built-in alpha channel(s) to control the compositing of the fg and bg inputs:

RGB = F+B(1-f) Alpha = f+b(1-f)

in.description: The input stream to be masked
The input stream to be masked

out.description: Output: the result of the mix operation

mix.description: The amount to mix bg to fg
A "mix" operation blending from "bg" to "fg" according to the mix amount, returning Fm+B(1-m)
mix.notes: See also the [Standard Shader Nodes](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.StandardNodes.md#standard-shader-nodes) section below for additional shader-semantic variants of the [mix node](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/MaterialX/blob/v1.39.5/documents/Specification/MaterialX.StandardNodes.md#node-mix-shader).

Output: copy input value to all channels
Output: an unlit surface shader emitting the input value as color

convert.description: Convert a stream from one data type to another.
convert.notes:
For boolean input values, all numeric output values will be either zero or one.

For colorN/vectorN to colorM/vectorM:

* if N is the same as M, then channels are directly copied. * if N is larger than M, then the first M channels are used and the excess channels ignored. * if N is smaller than M, then the N channels are directly copied and additional channels are populated with 0, aside from the fourth channel which is populated with 1.

index.description: The index of the channel in in to extract

in.description: The input matrix from which to extract a row

index.description: The index of the row in in to extract

out.description: Output: the indexth row of in as a vector

extract.description:
Isolate a single channel from a colorN, vectorN, or matrixNN stream.

When the input is a colorN or vectorN, the node extracts a single float component by index. When the input is a matrix33 or matrix44, the node extracts a row vector by index.
extract.notes: The valid range for index should be clamped to $[0,N)$ in the user interface, where N is the number of components (for vector/color inputs) or rows (for matrix inputs). index is a uniform, non-varying value. Any index values outside of the valid range should result in an error.

in.description: The input stream to be separated

outx.description: Output: the x channel of in

outy.description: Output: the y channel of in

separate2.description: Split the channels of a 2-channel stream into separate float outputs.
separate2.notes: For the vector2-input in, outx and outy correspond to the x- and y-components of in.

outz.description: Output: the z channel of in

separate3.description: Split the channels of a 3-channel stream into separate float outputs.
separate3.notes:
When the input in is a color3, outr, outg, and outb correspond to the r-, g-, and b-components of in, respectively.

When the input in is a vector3, outx, outy, and outz correspond to the x-, y-, and z-components of in, respectively.

outx.description: Output: the x channel of in

outy.description: Output: the y channel of in

outz.description: Output: the z channel of in

outw.description: Output: the w channel of in

separate4.description: Split the channels of a 4-channel stream into separate float outputs.
separate4.notes:
When the input in is a color4, outr, outg, outb, and outa correspond to the r-, g-, b-, and alpha components of in, respectively.

When the input in is a vector4, outx, outy, outz, and outw correspond to the x-, y-, z-, and w-components of in, respectively.

size.description: The size of the blur kernel in 0-1 UV space

filtertype.description: The spatial filter used in the blur

blur.description: Applies a convolution blur to the input stream.

scale.description: Multiplier applied to the in signal

texcoord.description: The texture coordinates that the heightfield gradient is computed with respect to

out.description: Output: tangent-space normal computed from in

heighttonormal.description: Convert a scalar height map to a tangent-space normal map of type vector3. The output normal map is encoded with all channels in the [0-1] range, enabling its storage in unsigned image formats.

in.type: float, colorN, vectorN, matrixNN, boolean, integer, string, filename

dot.description:
A no-op, which passes its input through to its output unchanged.

Users can use dot nodes to shape edge connection paths or provide documentation checkpoints in node graph layout UI's. Dot nodes may also pass uniform values from constant or other nodes with uniform="true" outputs to uniform inputs and tokens.
